title: linnaea borealis subsp. longiflora
intro: Linnaea borealis subsp. longiflora, also known as the twinflower, is a flowering plant species in the heath family, Ericaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The twinflower has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Linnaea borealis subsp. longiflora are typically made from its flowers or leaves and can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions.
